column1: No. of PatientsDENGUE CASE REPORTING During  the  19th  week,  1623  suspected  dengue  cases were  reported  across  24  districts,  compared  to  1479 cases in the previous week, reflecting an 9.7% increase. The  Western  Province  accounted  for  48.9%  of  total cases,  with the  Colombo  Municipal  Council (CMC) contributing 3.5%, the rest of Colombo District 19.9%, Gampaha  District  17.6%,  and  Kalutara  District  7.9%. Outside the Western Province, notable case contributions  were  reported  from  Ratnapura  (9.8%), Matara  (5.6%),  Kandy  (5.6%),  Galle  (4.3%),  Kegalle (3.6%), Kurunegala (3.5%), Batticaloa (2.9%), Hambantota (2.8%), Kalmunai (2.2%), and Trincomalee (2.2%) (see Table 1). Previously classified 13 high-risk MOH areas in week 18, were not identified as high-risk in the 19th week of 2026 (Table 2). MIDNIGHT TOTAL OF DENGUE PATIENT ADMISSIONS *  After  incorporating  delayed  hospital  reports,  the average midnight total for the 18th week increased from 500 to 508. In the 19th week of 2026, the average midnight total of dengue  patients  across  74  sentinel  hospitals  was  550. Compared  to  the  previous  week,  in  the  19th  week,  28 hospitals reported an increase,  while 19  hospitals reported a decrease.
